t_ch: 我还想知道……
t_en: I still want to know...

t_ch: 还有！来看看这是什么——仓鼠球骑士毛发护理师证！我才考到的。厉害吧！
t_en: There's more! Feast your eyes on this: A Hamster Ball Knight hairstylist cert! I just got it too. How about that!

t_ch: 收藏库里多了一张重要的证书，是不是说明我也变得更重要了？
t_en: Another important piece to add to my collection. Does that mean I've become even more important now?

t_ch: 应该是的吧。
t_en: I guess so.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我要把这张证书用金框裱起来，挂在房间最显眼的地方，让每个来玩的人都看得到！还要邀请家里所有长辈都来玩！
t_en: Heheh, I'm gonna frame this one up and show it off in the most conspicuous space in my room! Everyone who comes over will be able to see it! Oh, I have to invite my elders too!

t_ch: 我要让看到这张证书的人都知道，我是很厉害的！
t_en: I want all who set their eyes on this certificate to know that I'm THAT good!

t_ch: 没有关系吧。
t_en: I'm not sure it matters.

t_ch: 怎么会！要、要是没有证书，对方怎么会知道我是个厉害的人呢。要是不觉得我很厉害，就会欺负我，看不起我……
t_en: What're you saying! If-If I didn't have any certificates, how would others know I'm super awesome? And if they don't think so, they'll bully me and despise me...

t_ch: 不过你是好朋友！你肯定会照顾我的！
t_en: But you're my good friend! I bet you'll take real good care of me!

t_ch: 看看我的！
t_en: Watch this!

t_ch: 你不慌不忙地从口袋里掏出了梦境护照，颇为自豪地翻开展示。望着上面密密麻麻的贴纸，笨罐先生露出了羡慕的神情。
t_en: You steadily fish out a Dreamscape Pass from your pocket, showing it off like a prized possession. Scrutinizing the cluster of stickers on it, an envious expression plasters Mr. Piggy Bank's face.

t_ch: 我知道这个！是家族发给外来宾客的！好看。我也想要。
t_en: I know this one! It's what The Family gives to visitors! Nice. I want one too.

t_ch: 游览的景点越多，就能收集到越多贴纸，这、这是人生的记录，是知识和阅历的证明！
t_en: The more sights you see, the more stickers you can collect. This, this is a journal of your life, and it's proof of your rite of knowledge!

t_ch: 你去过好多地方，我好羡慕。虽然我也去过，但没有证据……
t_en: I'm so envious, as you've been to so many places. I mean, I visited these places too, but I have no proof...

t_ch: 感谢你的介绍。
t_en: Thank you for your introduction.

t_ch: 呼，和你聊天真开心，好久没人仔细听我说话了。我要给你颁发我制作的证书，称号就叫…就叫——”笨罐之友“！怎么样，是不是特气派！
t_en: Phew, it was fun talking to you. It's been a long time since anyone really listened to me so earnestly. I hereby award you with a certificate I created. It's called... Let's just call it "Piggy Bank's Friend"! How's that for a stylish name!

t_ch: 下次还要来和我分享证书的快乐啊。
t_en: Don't forget to return and share more fun with certificates with me.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 家里的长辈说…不能随便给人摸这个球。
t_en: My elders said... not to let strangers touch this ball.

t_ch: 不过，你愿意陪我聊天，还对证书感兴趣…唔，就让你轻轻碰一碰吧。
t_en: But, you're so willing to chat with me, and you're interested in certificates too... I'll let you touch it just a little.

t_ch: 你有些胆怯地伸出手，小心翼翼地将那团柔软的绒毛置于指尖…然后感受到了前所未有的舒适与安心感。你这时才明白，原来人与人的交流还可以如此愉快。
t_en: You cautiously reach out, carefully touching that soft fluffy ball with your fingertip... and then a feeling of unprecedented comfort and calm washes over you. It is at this moment that you finally realize how joyful it is to interact with people.

t_ch: 有点痒痒的，嘿嘿…是不是感觉还不错。
t_en: It tickles, heheh... I bet it feels good, doesn't it?

t_ch: 感谢你的介绍。
t_en: Thank you for your introduction.

t_ch: 呼，和你聊天真开心，好久没人仔细听我说话了。我要给你颁发我制作的证书，称号就叫…就叫——”笨罐之友“！怎么样，是不是特气派！
t_en: Phew, it was fun talking to you. It's been a long time since anyone really listened to me so earnestly. I hereby award you with a certificate I created. It's called... Let's just call it "Piggy Bank's Friend"! How's that for a stylish name!

t_ch: 下次还要来和我分享证书的快乐啊。
t_en: Don't forget to return and share more fun with certificates with me.

t_ch: 你被迫听笨罐先生讲解起了他获得证书的艰辛历程，时间从未流逝得如此缓慢。
t_en: You are forced to sit through Mr. Piggy Bank's monologue on his arduous journey to acquire all his certificates. Time trickles slower than your perception of time has ever fathomed.

t_ch: 球笼轨道维修资格、剧院报幕员证、训物大赛银奖、还有酒店下水道疏通技能证……
t_en: Spheroid rail repair, theater announcer, object training silver medal, and hotel drainage unclogging certificates...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，会耐心听我说的，你是第一个！好朋友！
t_en: Heheh, you're the first one who's ever had the patience to sit through my monologue! You're a good friend!

t_ch: 假的。没有意义。只是骗自己。有什么用……
t_en: It's fake. Meaningless. You're just lying to yourself. What's the point...

t_ch: 洋溢在笨罐先生脸上的那种天真无邪的笑意消失了，取而代之的是…你很熟悉的，享用完一顿绝妙午餐后的困倦与乏味。
t_en: That sweet, sunshine smile that Mr. Piggy Bank is brimming with vanishes, replaced by... that look you're all too familiar with: The lethargy and tedium you experience after a sumptuous lunch.

t_ch: 为什么会这么空虚，感觉就像…在喝一瓶完全没有汽的苏乐达。
t_en: Why does it feel so empty, like... drinking an uncarbonated SoulGlad.

t_ch: 有什么用啊。有什么用。收集这闪金光的玩意，就为了挂在家里，到处喊人来看？
t_en: What's the point... What's the point. Collecting all these shiny knick-knacks just to hang them at home, and then getting people to come over to look at them?

t_ch: 我就像是美梦剧团里拿肚子敲鼓的茶壶，烧头发照明的蜡烛，用愚蠢的行为逗人发笑，从掌声里博得可怜的存在感，还沾沾自喜以为得到了认同。
t_en: I am like that teapot in the Sweet Dreams Troupe that plays its own belly like a drum, and the candle that burns its own hair to light the room, employing silly antics to make people laugh, garnering meager ounces of worth from their applause, yet still revels in it as recognition.

t_ch: 假的。没有意义。只是骗自己。有什么用。
t_en: Fake. Meaningless. Delusional. Pointless.

t_ch: 可…我还能做什么？
t_en: But... What else can I do?

t_ch: 好朋友。给你。别担心，这瓶是有气的。
t_en: Good friend, here. Don't worry. This one's not flat.

t_ch: 为什么会这么空虚，感觉就像…在喝一瓶完全没有汽的苏乐达。
t_en: Why does it feel so empty, like... drinking an uncarbonated SoulGlad.

t_ch: 有什么用啊。有什么用。收集这闪金光的玩意，就为了挂在家里，到处喊人来看？
t_en: What's the point... What's the point. Collecting all these shiny knick-knacks just to hang them at home, and then getting people to come over to look at them?

t_ch: 我就像是美梦剧团里拿肚子敲鼓的茶壶，烧头发照明的蜡烛，用愚蠢的行为逗人发笑，从掌声里博得可怜的存在感，还沾沾自喜以为得到了认同。
t_en: I am like that teapot in the Sweet Dreams Troupe that plays its own belly like a drum, and the candle that burns its own hair to light the room, employing silly antics to make people laugh, garnering meager ounces of worth from their applause, yet still revels in it as recognition.

t_ch: 假的。没有意义。只是骗自己。有什么用。
t_en: Fake. Meaningless. Delusional. Pointless.

t_ch: 可…我还能做什么？
t_en: But... What else can I do?

t_ch: 我是橡木家系的派诺！我以我的姓氏为荣！
t_en: I am Paino of the Oak Family! I wear my family's name with pride!

t_ch: 我、我…不是废物！
t_en: I, I... am not worthless!

t_ch: 我、我…不是笨罐！我、我…有自己的名字！我的名字是…是……
t_en: I, I... am not Piggy Bank! I, I... have my own name! My name is... it is...

t_ch: 我是橡木家系的派诺！我以我的姓氏为荣！
t_en: I am Paino of the Oak Family! I wear my family's name with pride!

t_ch: 我知道，比起同辈的其他人，我没什么才能，性格又懦弱，不会说话，长得不好看，也不讨人喜欢，但是…但是，我也爱着我的家族，请你们也多看看我！
t_en: I know that compared to my peers, I lack talent, courage, eloquence, looks, pleasantness, but... But! I love my family too, so please pay more attention to me!

t_ch: 我不需要头衔，我只想要在餐桌上有个座位！想要在任何地方，都能大声说出自己的名字！想骄傲地，把刚认识的好朋友介绍给家里人！我、我……
t_en: I don't need titles. I just want a seat at the table! I want to go wherever I want and to yell out my own name loud and proud! I want to introduce my newfound friends to my family! I, I...

t_ch: 求求你们了……
t_en: Please...

t_ch: 呜呜…从来没人爱过我，就算有谁搭话，也只是想骗我……
t_en: *sob*... No one's ever loved me, and those who talk to me all have ulterior motives...

t_ch: 呜呜…从来没人爱过我，就算有谁搭话，也只是想骗我……
t_en: *sob*... No one's ever loved me, and those who talk to me all have ulterior motives...

t_ch: 大家都想要和橡木家系的成员套近乎，却没人想和笨罐先生做朋友。
t_en: Everyone just wants to cozy up to the Oak Family, but no one wants to be friends with Mr. Piggy Bank.

t_ch: 你呢？我的心灵之友。你和我聊天，该不会也是……
t_en: What about you? My kindred friend. You chatting with me, are you also...

t_ch: 我是你真正的朋友。
t_en: I am your true friend.

t_ch: 呜呜，谢谢你。你太好了。我、我也相信，你是我最好的朋友。
t_en: *sob* Thank you. You're too kind. I, I'd like to believe it too, that you're my best friend.

t_ch: 我是冲着家族来的。
t_en: I'm here for The Family.

t_ch: 啊。可那些真正有企图的人，都不会直接说出来。
t_en: Ah. But those who really have a motive won't admit it upfront.

t_ch: 我知道了。你是在开玩笑，想让我心情变好。呜呜，谢谢你。
t_en: I've got it. You're just joking, trying to get me to laugh.

t_ch: 我只是来喝汽水的。
t_en: I'm here to drink some soda.

t_ch: 给你，都给你…喜欢苏乐达的人，肯定不是坏人。
t_en: Take it, all of it... Someone who likes soda can't be a bad egg.

t_ch: 呜呜…从来没人爱过我，就算有谁搭话，也只是想骗我……
t_en: *sob*... No one's ever loved me, and those who talk to me all have ulterior motives...

t_ch: 大家都想要和橡木家系的成员套近乎，却没人想和笨罐先生做朋友。
t_en: Everyone just wants to cozy up to the Oak Family, but no one wants to be friends with Mr. Piggy Bank.

t_ch: 你呢？我的心灵之友。你和我聊天，该不会也是……
t_en: What about you? My kindred friend. You chatting with me, are you also...

t_ch: 我是你真正的朋友。
t_en: I am your true friend.

t_ch: 呜呜，谢谢你。你太好了。我、我也相信，你是我最好的朋友。
t_en: *sob* Thank you. You're too kind. I, I'd like to believe it too, that you're my best friend.

t_ch: 我是冲着家族来的。
t_en: I'm here for The Family.

t_ch: 啊。可那些真正有企图的人，都不会直接说出来。
t_en: Ah. But those who really have a motive won't admit it upfront.

t_ch: 我知道了。你是在开玩笑，想让我心情变好。呜呜，谢谢你。
t_en: I've got it. You're just joking, trying to get me to laugh.

t_ch: 跟你说了会儿话，感觉心里没那么堵了。但不知道为什么，我还是好想哭……
t_en: I feel much better after talking to you. But I don't know why, I still feel like crying...

t_ch: 不可能存在完美的事物，绝对有什么地方不对……
t_en: There's no such thing as a perfect thing. There's always a flaw...

t_ch: 您好呀，这儿的夜色可真美。多希望时针不要转动，我们能永远生活在一片祥和的梦里。
t_en: Hello, the night scenery's really a sight, isn't it? How I wish that time would stop, that we can forever live in this serene dream.

t_ch: 嗯？看您的打扮，应该不是家族的人吧？让我猜猜…幻想暴富的逐梦客，受邀前来的贵宾？还是说…您能给我更多惊喜呢？
t_en: Hmm? Looking at your attire, I don't suppose you're from The Family? Let me guess... a guest who was invited here? A Dreamchaser who fantasizes about striking it rich? Or are you... going to surprise me?

t_ch: 我是逐梦客。
t_en: I am a Dreamchaser.

t_ch: …骗人。
t_en: ...Liar.

t_ch: 逐梦客的类别五花八门，只有一点共通之处——他们全都擅长编织谎言。但我在您的身上嗅不到伪装的味道。
t_en: Those who chase dreams come from all walks of life, but they all have one thing in common — their deftness in spinning lies. But I do not sense even a tinge of hypocrisy on you.

t_ch: 越是能言善道，越不懂得享受沉默。您到现在都还没主动开口，想必也不是那种巧舌如簧，只晓得挑拨是非的人吧？
t_en: The more silver-tongued one is, the less they understand the joys of silence. Till now, you do not speak unless spoken to. I reckon you aren't the gab-gifted, fomenting sort?

t_ch: 我是家族的宾客。
t_en: I am a guest of The Family's.

t_ch: …真的？
t_en: ...Really?

t_ch: 不是怀疑您，只是最近冒充贵宾的人可太多了。瞧，猎犬家系的人正在那巡视呢，紧张兮兮的，怕是有什么大事要发生。
t_en: I do not doubt you, but there have been far too many imposters of late. Look, members of the Bloodhound Family are on patrol, anxiously on edge, as if something major is about to erupt.

t_ch: 您要是宾客的话，会知道些什么吗？
t_en: If you are a guest, what would you like to know?

t_ch: 星穹列车到匹诺康尼了！
t_en: The Astral Express has arrived at Penacony!

t_ch: 星穹列车？唔，是那群忙于开拓的无名客吧？很崇高，我很喜欢。
t_en: The Astral Express? Oh, that bunch of Nameless scurrying about with their Trailblazing? How admirable! I quite like that.

t_ch: 知更鸟居然是家族的人！
t_en: Robin is a member of The Family!?

t_ch: 我知道呀。我还知道那位小姐，和她的家人之间，似乎闹了点…小矛盾。
t_en: I knew that. I even know that lady has had some... disagreement with her Family.

t_ch: 光鲜亮丽的东西是没法长久保存的。您应该明白这个道理。
t_en: Splendid things can never be kept long. You should understand that.

t_ch: 钟表小子是真实存在的！
t_en: Clockie really exists!

t_ch: 您的意思是，那个电影里的，钟表小人…在现实里真的存在？
t_en: You mean, the character Mr. Pocket Watch from the movie... exists in reality?

t_ch: 还能和您交流，帮您做事，解决各种各样的事件，陪您一同冒险？
t_en: I can still talk to you, help you do stuff, solve all sorts of problems, and adventure with you?

t_ch: 作为无名客的奇妙经历或许很有趣。但不巧，我手头没有苏乐达，不然就可以安心听您讲故事了。
t_en: The wondrous sojourn of a Nameless could perhaps be interesting. Alas, I have no SoulGlad with me — an essential pairing when listening to your tale.

t_ch: 我就是传说中的钟表匠！
t_en: I am the fabled Watchmaker!

t_ch: 哈，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Ha, hahahaha.

t_ch: 抱歉，这个笑话很有趣——但我宁愿相信钟表小子真实存在。
t_en: Sorry, it's a funny joke, but I'd rather believe that Clockie really exists.

t_ch: 就不告诉你。
t_en: Not telling you.

t_ch: 就不告诉你。
t_en: Not telling you.

t_ch: …果然，您比我预想得还要有趣。
t_en: ...You are as interesting as I expected.

t_ch: 每个人都有秘密，每座城市都有它幽暗的角落。如果不想让秘密被暴露在大众的视野之下，可得多费一番功夫学习埋藏真相的技能。
t_en: Everyone has secrets, and every city has its dark corners. If its secrets are to be kept away from the public eye, you'd have to learn to be very skilled in burying the truth.

t_ch: 万一被预料之外的人给翻找出来，那场面可能会很难堪哦。
t_en: If someone you least expect uncovers them, it'll be an awkward sight.

t_ch: 嗯…看您一幅意犹未尽的样子，是还有什么别的事吗？
t_en: Hmm... You look like you have something else on your mind. What is it?

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 好呀。不过请别忘了，这是个梦的世界：您的所见所闻可都做不得真。
t_en: Sure. But don't forget, this is a dream world: Nothing you see or hear is real.

t_ch: 想知道你的秘密。
t_en: I want to know your secret.

t_ch: 行呀。您想知道些什么呢？
t_en: Sure. What do you want to know?

t_ch: 你的名字…
t_en: Your name...

t_ch: 呵呵，您不是知道的很清楚嘛？怀疑是假名的话，可以去询问酒店前台哦。
t_en: Heheh, I thought you already knew that? If you suspect that it's a fake name, you can ask the hotel reception.

t_ch: 相信我，家族可不是那么好骗的。
t_en: Believe me, The Family doesn't take kindly to lies.

t_ch: 你的来历…
t_en: Where you're from...

t_ch: 我来自很遥远的地方，您完全没听说过的荒凉星球，那里只有黑暗和无止境的劳作，人们只有拼尽全力才能在自然的夹缝中勉强活下去。
t_en: I come from a faraway place, from a barren planet that you've never heard of, where there is only darkness and endless labor. Our people have to work themselves to the bone to eke a living in the crevices of nature.

t_ch: 相信我，那不是个美好的故事…大概不符合您的审美，所以我就省下这个口舌了。
t_en: Believe me, it's not a pretty story... and probably not a great fit for your aesthetics. I'll leave it at that.

t_ch: 你来匹诺康尼的目的…
t_en: Your reason for coming to Penacony...

t_ch: 和您在这遇见的大部份人一样。享受，发财…谁能拒绝美好的梦呢？
t_en: Not that different from most of the people you've met here. To enjoy, to make a fortune... Who can withstand the allure of a beautiful dream?

t_ch: 明知梦境会破灭，却始终不愿迎接现实的阳光……
t_en: Knowing that dreams will get crushed, yet still welcoming the blinding rays of reality with open arms...

t_ch: 相信我，您很快也会爱上这里的。
t_en: Believe me, you'll fall in love with this place in no time.

t_ch: 没什么想了解的。
t_en: There's nothing else I'd like to ask about.

t_ch: 想邀请你出去玩。
t_en: I'd like to ask you out to have some fun.

t_ch: 呀！好突然。我可以把这句话，当作是友好的邀约吗？
t_en: Ooh! How bold. Dare I take this request as a friendly invite?

t_ch: 事先问问，您想去什么地方游览呢？
t_en: First things first: Where would you like to visit?

t_ch: 梦境贩售店？
t_en: The Dreamscape Sales Store?

t_ch: 那可是个好地方，能体验到形形色色、在现实中绝对没法接触的生活。
t_en: That's a good place. You can get to experience the vibrant sights, and revel in a lifestyle you wouldn't otherwise have in real life.

t_ch: 不过…店里的梦泡，我都已经观看过了。
t_en: But... I've already seen the dream bubbles in the store.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼大剧院？
t_en: The Penacony Grand Theater?

t_ch: 那座剧院可太棒了！我在现实里从没见过这么宏伟的建筑，这是只属于梦境的奇迹，所有人都该去那看看。
t_en: The theater is amazing! I've never seen such a grand building in real life. It's a wonder only reserved for the Dreamscape — everyone should go see it once.

t_ch: 不过…我才刚在那看完表演，现在有点累了。抱歉。
t_en: But... I just got done watching a performance there, and I'm a little tired. Sorry.

t_ch: 酒店的白金套房？
t_en: The hotel's Platinum Suite?

t_ch: 那些客房真的很迷人，对吧？居然能把整个星球都打造成酒店，这份想象力和执着的决心可太了不起了。
t_en: Those rooms are mesmerizing, no? To turn the entire planet into a hotel — that type of creativity and determination are to be admired.

t_ch: 不过…我想在外面透透气，还不打算回去。抱歉。
t_en: But... I prefer to take a breather outside, so I'm not ready to return yet. Sorry.

t_ch: 没什么想去的地方。
t_en: I have nowhere that I want to go.

t_ch: 没什么想法。
t_en: I don't have any ideas.

t_ch: 唔，都结束了？那到我的提问环节了，您不介意吧？
t_en: Boo, over already? It's my turn to ask questions then. I hope you don't mind?

t_ch: …依我的观察，您不是本地人，也不像抱着发财愿望前来的逐梦客；既没有沉陷在香甜的夜色中，也对向上爬不感兴趣。您有自己的想法。
t_en: ...From my observations, you aren't local, and don't resemble any of those Dreamchasers who harbor fantasies of fortune. You're not caught in the sights of night, and have no interest in climbing the social ladder — you've got other plans.

t_ch: 最重要的…您并不信任家族，对这个梦抱有疑虑。
t_en: Most importantly... You don't trust The Family, and harbor disbelief in the dream.

t_ch: 那么，您希望获悉和这个梦境有关的真相吗？
t_en: Then, you wish to obtain the truth to this Dreamscape?

t_ch: 希望。
t_en: I do.

t_ch: 不希望。
t_en: I don't.

t_ch: 我不知道…
t_en: I don't know...

t_ch: 原来如此…我明白了。
t_en: I see... I get it.

t_ch: 无论如何，我都会祝您旅途顺利——哦，还有…如果我是您的话，就会尽量避免和「调查记者辛格勒」产生联系。
t_en: Regardless, I bid you safe journey. Oh, and... If I were you, I'd steer clear of Singler, that investigative reporter.

t_ch: 毕竟，谁也不希望在享受梦境的时候惹上麻烦…您说对吧？
t_en: I mean, no one wants to get into trouble while they're enjoying their dreams... Don't you think?

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: …您莫非…是在开玩笑？
t_en: ...Could you be... pulling my leg?

t_ch: 所有生活在匹诺康尼的人都明白，向一位皮皮西女性提出这种要求是极其无礼的行为。若非您先前的表现始终很礼貌，我都要怀疑是不是遇上了偷渡过来的罪犯。
t_en: All who live on Penacony know that it is extremely rude to extend such a request to a Pepeshi lady. If you weren't on your best behavior just now, I would've suspected you were some fugitive on the lam.

t_ch: 好吧…我必须承认，您确实带来了惊喜。
t_en: Fine... I have to admit that you have surprised me.

t_ch: 我没有问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 原来如此…我明白了。
t_en: I see... I get it.

t_ch: 无论如何，我都会祝您旅途顺利——哦，还有…如果我是您的话，就会尽量避免和「调查记者辛格勒」产生联系。
t_en: Regardless, I bid you safe journey. Oh, and... If I were you, I'd steer clear of Singler, that investigative reporter.

t_ch: 毕竟，谁也不希望在享受梦境的时候惹上麻烦…您说对吧？
t_en: I mean, no one wants to get into trouble while they're enjoying their dreams... Don't you think?

t_ch: 糖浆令人沉醉，夜色惹人着迷。不知是什么让您如此眷恋难忘，迟迟不愿离去？
t_en: The syrup inebriates the tongue, and the night entrances the eyes. I wonder what prevents your heart from forgetting, to linger?

t_ch: 每个人都有秘密，每座城市都有它幽暗的角落。如果不想让秘密被暴露在大众的视野之下，可得多费一番功夫学习埋藏真相的技能。
t_en: Everyone has secrets, and every city has its dark corners. If its secrets are to be kept away from the public eye, you'd have to learn to be very skilled in burying the truth.

t_ch: 万一被预料之外的人给翻找出来，那场面可能会很难堪哦。
t_en: If someone you least expect uncovers them, it'll be an awkward sight.

t_ch: 梦里的世界如此美妙，可这不该是真的。完美背后总有代价。
t_en: The world in the dream is splendid indeed, but none of it is real. There is always a price behind such beauty.

t_ch: 我想找到这些，我想让真相公之于众。哪怕会将美好全部砸碎，落得满身骂名。
t_en: When I think of such things, I can't help but want to bring the truth to bear, even if I have to shatter its beauty, to shoulder the scorn of many.

t_ch: 不知为何，辛格勒倏忽间陷入了沉默。她的眉眼低垂，似要吐露某个欲说还休的秘密。
t_en: For some reason, Singler suddenly sinks into silence. Her brows are sullen, as if she is about to spill some forbidden secret.

t_ch: 您是值得信任的对象，我想…把自己的真实身份告诉您。这样，即使有一天我永远地从梦中消失，您也不会感到意外。
t_en: You are a trustworthy person, and I think... I want to tell you my real identity. That way, even if I forever disappear from the dream, you will not be surprised.

t_ch: 我其实是一名调查记者。之所以来到匹诺康尼，是因为我想揭穿藏在美梦表象之下的阴谋。
t_en: I am an investigative reporter. My presence on Penacony is to reveal the conspiracy behind this facade of splendor.

t_ch: 以我对这个宇宙运行规则的理解——塑造「完美」的事物往往需要付出巨大的代价。在匹诺康尼的家族背后，恐怕深藏着某些不为人知的丑恶罪行。
t_en: According to my understanding of the rules of this universe, the creation of such perfection always requires a hefty price. Lurking in the closets of The Family, I fear there are unknown sinister acts in motion.

t_ch: 我想发掘出那些罪行，然后把真相公之于众。哪怕需要将这虚构的美好全部砸碎，哪怕这会引起很多人的不满。
t_en: I want to unveil these misdeeds, and present the truth to the public, even if I have to completely shatter this facade of brilliance, even if I invoke the wrath of many.

t_ch: 但是…呵，您肯定明白，每个人都有自己的使命和坚持。而我坚持的东西恰巧名叫「真相」。
t_en: But... Heh, you must understand, everyone has their own calling and tenacity. And mine is coincidentally fueled by the "truth."

t_ch: 您已经知道我的秘密了…如果有一天我从梦境中永远地消失了，希望您也不要感到意外。
t_en: You already know my secret... If I forever disappear from the Dreamscape, I hope you will not be surprised.

t_ch: 我是一名调查记者。之所以来到匹诺康尼，是因为我想揭穿藏在美梦表象之下的阴谋。
t_en: I am an investigative reporter. My presence on Penacony is to reveal the conspiracy behind this facade of splendor.

t_ch: 以我对这个宇宙运行规则的理解——塑造「完美」的事物往往需要付出巨大的代价。在匹诺康尼的家族背后，恐怕深藏着某些不为人知的丑恶罪行。
t_en: According to my understanding of the rules of this universe, the creation of such perfection always requires a hefty price. Lurking in the closets of The Family, I fear there are unknown sinister acts in motion.

t_ch: 我想发掘出那些罪行，然后把真相公之于众。哪怕需要将这虚构的美好全部砸碎，哪怕这会引起很多人的不满。
t_en: I want to unveil these misdeeds, and present the truth to the public, even if I have to completely shatter this facade of brilliance, even if I invoke the wrath of many.

t_ch: 但是…呵，您肯定明白，每个人都有自己的使命和坚持。而我坚持的东西恰巧名叫「真相」。
t_en: But... Heh, you must understand, everyone has their own calling and tenacity. And mine is coincidentally fueled by the "truth."

t_ch: …哼，总有天，我要把家族的底全扒出来，丢到所有人面前，再踩上几脚。
t_en: ...Hmph, one day I'll dig up everything on The Family, throw it at the feet of everyone, and even trample on it for measure.

t_ch: 让做梦的人看清楚，这繁华背后究竟有多不堪！
t_en: I'll show those Dreamweavers the ugly truth behind all this wealth!

t_ch: 那些家族的野狗，到现在都不肯放过我！
t_en: Those rabid dogs of The Family are always on my tail!

t_ch: 你目瞪口呆地看辛格勒从包里扯出一张报纸，纸面宽大得足以遮住她的半个身子。好吧，看来身材不那么高大确有好处。
t_en: Dumbfounded, you see Singler whip out a newspaper from her bag. It is wide enough to cover half her body. Sometimes being vertically challenged has its advantages.

t_ch: 把头埋低，别让他们看到您的脸。啧。都是流氓，恶棍，毫无怜悯心的暴徒！
t_en: Keep your head down, and don't let them see your face. Tsk. Louts, ruffians, scoundrels, cold-hearted scoundrels, all of them!

t_ch: 前天在筑梦边境，我差点就抓住了他们的尾巴。可那群野狗突然冲出来，声称我在盗窃商业机密，顺手还砸了我的相机！这就是匹诺康尼的待客之道吗？！
t_en: Two days ago, at the Dream's Edge, I almost caught them slipping up. But those rabid dogs suddenly rushed me, claiming that I was stealing trade secrets! They even smashed my camera! Is that how Penacony guests are treated!?

t_ch: 从那时起，不管走到哪，总能看见有人跟着我…您瞧，那边几个假装在等车的就是！
t_en: From that day on, no matter where I went, I would notice people following me... Look, like those few people pretending to be waiting for pick-ups!

t_ch: …哼，总有天，我要把家族的底全扒出来，丢到所有人面前，再踩上几脚。
t_en: ...Hmph, one day I'll dig up everything on The Family, throw it at the feet of everyone, and even trample on it for measure.

t_ch: 让做梦的人看清楚，这繁华背后究竟有多不堪！
t_en: I'll show those Dreamweavers the ugly truth behind all this wealth!

t_ch: 毫无进展…家族的手脚不可能这么干净，肯定会留下痕迹……
t_en: I'm getting nowhere... The Family's hands can't be this clean, and there must be a clue somewhere...

t_ch: 到底是什么地方…突破口在哪……
t_en: Where exactly is that place... What am I missing...

t_ch: 毫无进展…家族的手脚不可能这么干净，肯定会留下痕迹……
t_en: I'm getting nowhere... The Family's hands can't be this clean, and there must be a clue somewhere...

t_ch: 尽管悲哀的模样因人而异，但像这样会疯狂在速记本上写稿的…你还是第一次见。
t_en: While the look of tragedy varies among people, this is the first person you've met who writes on the notepad as if they're demon-possessed...

t_ch: 到底是什么地方…突破口在哪…星期日？那个人绝对有问题，但行事严密，抓不出破绽…知更鸟？那只小鸟知道些什么，可她同样不好对付……
t_en: Where could it be... What am I missing... Sunday? Something's definitely off with that person, but all his business is watertight, and I can't find any issues... Robin? What does that little bird know? And she's not a trifle to handle either...

t_ch: 我的直觉…难道出错了吗？我资深调查记者的美名就要葬送在这了吗……
t_en: Are my instincts... Wrong? Will this prolific investigative reporter meet her demise here...

t_ch: 啊啊…我的积蓄只够在酒店住半个月了，后面怎么办…难道要我去帮忙洗盘子拖地吗？
t_en: Aahhh... I only have enough savings to stay in this hotel for two more weeks. What do I do after that... Will I have to help with washing the dishes and mopping the floor?

t_ch: 要是再拿不出像样的选题，我肯定会被报社开除的……
t_en: If I can't come up with any decent headlines, the agency will surely fire me...

t_ch: 您…您身上不会刚好有些闲钱吧？您介意支持一下正义的调查事业吗？
t_en: You... Do you have any dispensable cash on you? Care to support righteous investigative journalism?

t_ch: 小捐一笔。
t_en: I'll donate a little.

t_ch: 啊…谢谢您，虽然不多，但如果省吃俭用的话也足够我再多撑几天了。
t_en: Ah... Thank you. It's not much, but I can hold out for a few more days if I scrimp and save.

t_ch: 感谢您支持我的事业…这点礼物不成心意，还请您收下。
t_en: Thank you for supporting my work... This token gift may not be much, but please take it.

t_ch: 都拿去花！
t_en: Take it all!

t_ch: 这、这么多？！
t_en: S—So much!?

t_ch: 我…我真不知道该怎么感谢您才好。这笔钱应该足够我在匹诺康尼多待好一阵了。我一定会继续争分夺秒、挖掘真相……
t_en: I... I really don't know how to thank you. This money is enough for me to stay on Penacony for a good while. I will keep racing against time to uncover the truth...

t_ch: 感谢您支持我的事业…这点礼物不成心意，还请您收下。
t_en: Thank you for supporting my work... This token gift may not be much, but please take it.

t_ch: 我实在不想掺和这事。
t_en: I really don't want to get involved.

t_ch: 这样啊…我能理解，毕竟大家都是来享受梦境世界的欢乐氛围的…有谁会在意那所谓的真相呢？
t_en: Is that so... I understand, everyone's here to enjoy the Dreamscape's happy vibes... Who'd ever care about the truth?

t_ch: 您请回吧，我需要些时间整理下思绪……
t_en: Please leave. I need some time to organize my thoughts...

t_ch: 毫无进展…家族的手脚不可能这么干净，肯定会留下痕迹……
t_en: I'm getting nowhere... The Family's hands can't be this clean, and there must be a clue somewhere...

t_ch: 到底是什么地方…突破口在哪…星期日？那个人绝对有问题，但行事严密，抓不出破绽…知更鸟？那只小鸟知道些什么，可她同样不好对付……
t_en: Where could it be... What am I missing... Sunday? Something's definitely off with that person, but all his business is watertight, and I can't find any issues... Robin? What does that little bird know? And she's not a trifle to handle either...

t_ch: 我的直觉…难道出错了吗？我资深调查记者的美名就要葬送在这了吗……
t_en: Are my instincts... Wrong? Will this prolific investigative reporter meet her demise here...

t_ch: …怎么回事，风扇声音这么大。
t_en: ...What's going on? The fan's too loud.

t_ch: 你被马达轰鸣般的沉闷声响所吸引，但出现在视线里的不过是位——以对匹诺康尼宾客的评判标准而言——平平无奇的智械男性。
t_en: You are attracted by the low hum of what sounds like a whirring motor, but what you come across is — by the lofty standards of Penacony's guests — a bland-looking Intellitron man.

t_ch: 不愧是上个技术世代的引擎，在体内运转起来的感觉就是不同，很顺畅，像是星鲸在歌唱。声响是大了点，但不重要，只要型号合适，散热降噪都不成问题。
t_en: Got to hand it to engines made with old-gen tech. Feels very different when it's running, very smooth. Like a starwhale's song. A bit louder than usual, but that's not important — as long as the model's compatible, heat dispersion and noise reduction aren't problems.

t_ch: 你好。站了这么久，是对旧机器感兴趣吗？我这里应有尽有，可以随便观看。
t_en: Greetings. You've been standing there for a while. Interested in old machines? I've got everything you need. Feel free to browse.

t_ch: 一台积满了灰尘的脏电脑。
t_en: A dirty computer coated with dust.

t_ch: 那是最初的原型机，还能勉强使用，现在市面上已经找不到了，据说能够破解某些特殊加密的信息。曾经有个穿白大褂的男人来找我购买，被我回绝了。
t_en: That's the earliest prototype. Barely usable, it can't be found on the market now. It's touted to be able to crack some special encrypted data. Some man in a flowy white robe wanted to buy it off me, but I refused.

t_ch: 一块爬满了绿锈的电路板。
t_en: A circuit board layered with silicon-green rust.

t_ch: 这个…我记得是从早期的游戏卡带里拆下来的，那时候还没有虚拟技术，想要玩游戏，得把这个插到机器里才行。同期推出的还有把实体的剑，可惜没人知道它的下落。
t_en: This... I remember removing this off some early-era game cartridge. Virtual tech didn't exist at that time, so if you wanted to play a game, you'd have to stick this in a game console. There was some physical sword that rolled out and promoted at the same time — a pity no one knows its whereabouts.

t_ch: 一碗过期了许多年的泡面。
t_en: A bowl of instant noodles, years past its expiry date.

t_ch: 即使是生产工序高度标准化的杯面，也会随着地域和时代变化而产生差异，匹诺康尼的口味偏甜，冰冷星球的味道就会偏辣。因此，再普通的商品也有收藏的价值。
t_en: Even if cup noodles are mass-produced with stringent factory standards, they'd still differ depending on their geography and times. Penacony's tastes are mildly sweet, while Belobog's are slightly spicier. That's why even everyday goods such as these have collector's value.

t_ch: …你吃过泡面？
t_en: ...You've eaten instant noodles before?

t_ch: 味觉模组会帮我解析有机生命消费的「食物」，再通过模拟出来的神经元信号让我了解它的「味道」。
t_en: My taste module helps me analyze this "food" that organic entities consume; the neuronal signals simulated allow me to understand its "taste."

t_ch: 所以，没错，我知道泡面是什么味道。请你收起对无机生命体的刻板印象。
t_en: So, yes, I know what instant noodles taste like. Please keep your stereotypical impressions of inorganic life to yourself.

t_ch: 我就随便看看。
t_en: I'm just looking around.

t_ch: …还想看看吗？我的藏品还有许多。
t_en: ...Want to see more? I've got plenty of goodies.

t_ch: 你的收藏品出售吗？
t_en: Are your collectibles for sale?

t_ch: 我相信，只要价格足够公道，没有什么是非卖品。
t_en: I believe that, as long as the price is fair, everything's for sale.

t_ch: 收集这些有什么用呢？
t_en: What's the point of collecting these?

t_ch: …看来你完全不明白智械的处境。技术永远在发展，但总有人会被时代丢下。等到哪天，我身上的部件过时了、需要更换了，可市面上却找不到适配的型号该怎么办？
t_en: ...It appears you don't understand an Intellitron's situation at all. Technology is always advancing, but some will be left behind by the times. When that day comes, the parts within me will be outdated and need to be changed. But what if the market no longer has compatible models available?

t_ch: 有机生命的身体结构趋于稳定，往往在成百上千个琥珀纪的时间周期内都不会发生变化；即使面对病痛，多数情况下也只要服用药物就能解决问题。
t_en: Organic life structure is stable — it will not change over the thousands of Amber Eras. Even if struck by sickness, many of their conditions can be resolved with medicine.

t_ch: 但智械不同。我们被迫追赶科技发展的浪潮，时刻担心会不会有哪天，自己的身体机能会跟不上时代。
t_en: Intellitrons are different. We have been forced to pursue the tides of technological advancement. We always fear the day when our components fall behind the times.

t_ch: 万一真有被淘汰的那天，这些收藏就是我活下去的希望。
t_en: If there comes such a day when we are obsolete, these collectibles will be my hope to live on.

t_ch: 不用了，谢谢。
t_en: I don't need anything. Thank you.

t_ch: 没关系。这些不是你需要在意的事。
t_en: It's fine. You need not be concerned about such things.

t_ch: 留着总是能派上用场的。
t_en: There's always value in keeping these around.

t_ch: 不经意间，旧东西都已经堆了这么多了啊……
t_en: I collected a stockpile of this old stuff before I knew it...

t_ch: 技术的发展不等人啊。
t_en: The advancement of technology waits for no one.

t_ch: 前段时间，我去探望了几个老朋友，都是智械，上了年纪，许多年没见了。
t_en: I went to visit a couple of old friends recently. They're all Intellitrons entering ages of rust, whom I hadn't seen for a long time.

t_ch: 其中就有位…左腿膝盖的轴承碎掉了，找不到适配的更换零件，只能拿相似型号替代着。走路还勉强，跑和跳是不行了。没办法，活得太久，制造工艺太老，早该被淘汰了。
t_en: One of them... had a busted bearing in his left knee, and he couldn't find a suitable part. He had to settle for a lateral model as replacement. He had a hard time walking, and couldn't even run or jump. Nothing we can do — when you've lived too long, your make becomes too old, and you realize you should've been retired a long time ago.

t_ch: 技术发展是好事，但有的时候，真希望能等一等我们啊……
t_en: Technological advancement is a good thing, but sometimes, I really wish it could wait for us just a little...

t_ch: 我讨厌那些自说自话的研究员！
t_en: I hate those self-absorbed researchers!

t_ch: 就是群技术疯子！只顾着研发时候寻求刺激，根本不考虑用户感受！
t_en: It's those technofreaks! They only care about the thrills of research, never stopping to consider the feelings of the users!

t_ch: 产品更新迭代得飞快，关键的向下兼容却一点都不做，过几年就没法用了，非逼着我们不断去换零件。嘴里说着为科技献身，实际就是挤牙膏！想多卖几个钱！
t_en: Product updates iterate lightning-quick, but they do nothing about backwards compatibility — older parts become obsolete after a few years, and we're forced to keep changing parts. All that talk about sacrificing their all for science — it's all nothing but squeezing out a few more coins from consumer wallets!

t_ch: 旧型号越堆越多，都是那群开发人员的错！
t_en: It's all the fault of the developers' that the old models are getting piled high!

t_ch: …斐迪南德沉浸在美妙的幻想中了。
t_en: ...Ferdinand is deep in his own beautiful fantasy.

t_ch: 不知为何，斐迪南德的眼神开始迷离。他的神情安详，似是在回味某个美好而悠长的梦。
t_en: For some reason, Ferdinand's eyes start glazing over. His expression is serene, as if he is reminiscing on some sweet, distant dream.

t_ch: 啊，把我淹没吧…浪潮般涌来的崭新技术、闪闪发光的全新部件…若是要用有机生命的话语来打比方，那暗金色的润滑油就如流淌的奶与蜜般甘甜……
t_en: Ah, just drown me... cutting-edge technology that surges forth like a tide, shiny new parts... If I had to compare it in the words of organics, that lustrous golden lubricant is as smooth as silken milk, sweet as honeyed nectar...

t_ch: 迤逦灯火幽微，帷幕下影子沉睡~♪颓靡月夜…嗯？
t_en: The meandering lights faint, the shadows slumber 'neath the curtaaaaain ♪ on a downcast moonlit night... Hmm?

t_ch: 您好呀，这里可真美，不是吗？您瞧，从这里望过去——
t_en: Hello, it's pretty here, isn't it? Look, out in the distance...

t_ch: 您看见那些筑梦师了吗？就是他们将现实的荒漠敲碎，挑拣出形状姣好的碎片，又藉由想象与灵感为其涂抹艳色，最终精心编织成这个有序而稳定的梦之城。
t_en: You see those Dreamweavers? It is they who meticulously selected beautifully-shaped shards from the shattered remains of reality, then painted them with their imagination and inspiration, finally weaving them into this city of dreams, steadfast and orderly.

t_ch: 很有趣，对吧？简直就像在撰写一部精妙绝伦的小说——脱颖于现实，自由却并不狂乱，又因剔除了冗杂无章而显得愈发剔透动人。
t_en: Interesting, no? Like writing an elegantly compelling story — removed from reality, free but devoid of chaos, rendered even more exquisite by stripping down the disorderly impurities.

t_ch: 呀…自顾自说了这么多，实在是失礼了。您好，我是克劳迪娅，一名业余小说家。
t_en: Ah... excuse my discourtesy for hogging the conversation. Hello, I am Claudia, a freelance novelist.

t_ch: 希望刚刚并未给您带来困扰，请见谅……
t_en: I hope I didn't make you uneasy just now. Please forgive me...

t_ch: 没事，我喜欢聊天。
t_en: It's fine. I like to chat.

t_ch: 非常感谢您的体谅，不过与陌生人交流确实是件非常有趣的事。
t_en: Thank you for your patience. Speaking to strangers is indeed a joyous activity.

t_ch: 您看，星际之间人们南来北往，明明各自迥然不同，却时常因某些共性短暂地聚集在一起，期间又要在这并不牢靠的共性之中谋求更多共性以实现自己的目的……
t_en: You see, people of the stars hustle and bustle about, their journeys and destinations obviously vastly different. Yet, thanks to some inexplicable universality, they are usually brought together briefly. During which, they seek further resonance in this unreliable universality to realize their own goals...

t_ch: 简单来说呢，就是人们相遇便会有故事；而了解他们的故事，才能创作出更有趣的故事。您说是吧？
t_en: Simply put, interactions must be encountered to create stories. And it is through understanding their stories, that one is able to create even more compelling tales. Don't you think so?

t_ch: 你都写过什么小说？
t_en: What sort of novels have you written?

t_ch: 只是一些很小众的冒险小说罢了。其实我更想投身于民俗与怪谈的创作…只是笔力与阅历都尚浅，还需多加磨砺。
t_en: Just some mildly popular adventure novels. I wanted to dive into folklore and supernatural themes... But my wordsmithing and experiences lack substance, and require more practice.

t_ch: 不过嘛…您若是对此类题材感兴趣的话，我倒是推荐您读一读埃德加•卡特的作品，他的故事有种难以言喻的美感，相信您会喜欢的。
t_en: But... If you're interested in such topics, I can recommend you read Edgar Carter's works. His stories have an inexplicable beauty to them — I trust you will like them.

t_ch: 说起来…无意冒犯，但您身上有一种…非常特殊的气质。硬要比喻的话，大概就像冰一样坚硬清透、像雪一样柔软纯白，但在那冰封雪藏之下……
t_en: Speaking of... I mean no offense, but you have a sort of... very special air about you. If I had to describe it, it'd be clear and solid as ice, soft and ivory as snow, but buried beneath that frost...

t_ch: 如果您不介意的话，可以和我聊聊您的经历吗？
t_en: If you don't mind, can you tell me about your experiences?

t_ch: 在少女的盛情之下，你简单讲了些关于星穹列车的故事。
t_en: Spurred by the girl's enthusiasm, you briefly recount a few anecdotes about the Astral Express.

t_ch: 于是你看到那双原本盛满暮色的眼睛里渐渐亮起了群星。
t_en: And it is then that you see those once dusk-clouded eyes slowly flicker and sparkle like star clusters.

t_ch: 这就是无名客吗…能这般随心所欲地遨游星空，听起来可真浪漫。
t_en: So these are Nameless... To travel the stars to your hearts' desire, it sounds rather romantic.

t_ch: 要不是家里还有个不省心的哥哥和大堆产业要打理，我真想现在就请求和您一起去旅行。
t_en: If I didn't have a worry-wart brother and a ton of business to attend to, I would really like to ask you to take me with you.

t_ch: 虽然之前也时不时从博识学会的人手里收购些稀罕的消息作为写作素材，但那群学究死板得很，与您聊天就有意思多了。
t_en: While I occasionally get some rare bits of detail from the Intelligentsia Guild to use as material, those stuffy researchers really do bore me. It's much more fun to talk to you.

t_ch: 所以求求您啦，再多和我说一些您的故事吧？
t_en: So, could you please tell me a few more of your stories?

t_ch: 你想听什么？
t_en: What would you like to listen to?

t_ch: 您就给我讲讲之前都到过哪些地方、见闻过何等趣事吧？
t_en: Tell me about the places you've been to, the sights you've seen, interesting events like that?

t_ch: 讲讲黑塔空间站的事。
t_en: Talk about Herta Space Station.

t_ch: 你说起浩瀚宇宙中那艘智慧的孤舟，说起它那古怪的主人与众多人偶。
t_en: You talk about that lone ark coasting along myriad stars, about its eccentric master and her numerous puppets.

t_ch: 无论是这座空间站还是它的主人，我都早已久闻大名，只是一直尚未找到机会前去探访一番。
t_en: Be it this space station or its master, I've long heard about their fame. I just haven't found a chance to pay a visit.

t_ch: 不过听了您的描述，比起琳琅满目的奇物，我倒是觉得那些黑塔人偶和忆泡更有趣些。
t_en: But after hearing your elaboration, compared to the dazzling Curios of wonder, these Herta puppets and the Memory Bubbles are far more interesting.

t_ch: 人偶、幻觉、记忆…多好的题材，嗯…下个短篇就叫《人偶之梦》吧，您觉得如何？
t_en: Puppets, illusions, memories... So many themes. Mm... I'll name my next short chapter A Dream of Puppets. What do you think?

t_ch: 讲讲雅利洛-VI的事。
t_en: Talk about Jarilo-VI.

t_ch: 你说起冰雪中的贝洛伯格、不见天日的下城区，说起被星核迷惑的大守护者。
t_en: You talk about the snow-laden Belobog, the Underworld that never sees daylight, and the Supreme Guardian led astray by the Stellaron.

t_ch: 末日孤星之上，人们在永夜寒冬中挣扎求生，真好啊…啊，您别误会，我不是说这场灾难好，而是这个故事实在是个创作的绝佳题材。
t_en: On this dystopian lone planet, its people struggle for survival beneath an unending winter night. How nice... Ah, don't misunderstand. I don't revel in misfortune, just that this is the perfect theme for a story.

t_ch: 您看，当处于绝境之中时，人性的善恶往往会被放大到极致。在这样环境的重压下，人物的弧光与张力很容易便能够得到充分的体现…我已经忍不住想要开始构思故事了。
t_en: You see, in desperate plights, humanity's morality will be amplified to its extremes. In such a harsh environment, character development arcs and conflicts are more easily expressed... I already can't wait to start structuring this story.

t_ch: 讲讲仙舟「罗浮」的事。
t_en: Talk about the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 你说起巡猎与丰饶的宿怨，说起星核祸种、建木生发，说起绝灭大君的野心与诡诈。
t_en: You talk about the feud between The Hunt and Abundance, about the Stellaron seeding ruin through the ship, the Ambrosial Arbor's growth, the ambition and treachery of the Lord Ravager.

t_ch: 这…这故事简直比小说还要精彩、还要宏大！
t_en: This... This story is simply far more thrilling and awesome than a mere novel!

t_ch: 说起来…您觉得星神究竟是什么呢？祂们强大、神秘，凡人在其面前脆弱渺小得彷如蝼蚁…可祂们之间也会往来争斗、也有爱恨情仇。
t_en: Speaking of which... What do you think the Aeons are exactly? All-powerful, mysterious, where mortals are no more than gnats to THEM... But even among THEMSELVES, there is conflict, love, hate, revenge, a cocktail of emotions.

t_ch: 我曾看过许多以此为题材的作品。有时候我觉得祂们和我们没什么不同，只是更加强大而已；有时候我又会觉得祂们是某种不可名状的恐怖、不可窥探的疯狂。
t_en: I've read many works using this theme as a template. Sometimes I think THEY are not so different from us, just more powerful. And sometimes I think THEY are some undescribable horror, and some unfathomable madness.

t_ch: 但偶尔我也会不禁思考，或许这二者之间本就并不存在本质上的区别…您觉得呢？
t_en: But I also can't help but think: These two portrayals are really two sides of the same coin... What do you think?

t_ch: 就先聊到这吧。
t_en: Let's end it here for now.

t_ch: 能与您交谈真是一件幸事。
t_en: It was my good fortune to be able to speak with you.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: …即便是您，开这种玩笑也有些过分了。
t_en: ...That's a jest too far, even for you.

t_ch: 还是说…您其实并不了解这种行为究竟意味着什么呢？
t_en: Or... do you actually not really understand what this is all about?

t_ch: 确实不太了解…
t_en: I don't really understand...

t_ch: 抱歉，下次不会了。
t_en: Sorry, I won't do it next time.

t_ch: 呼…您听好——对皮皮西人而言，摸绒球是一种非常私人且亲昵的行为。
t_en: *sigh* Listen up: To a Pepeshi native, touching the fluff ball is an act that is extremely private and intimate.

t_ch: 虽然我知道您并非那般轻浮之人，但请千万不要再提出这样的请求了哦。
t_en: I know you're not the careless type, but I would implore that you don't ask to do this ever again.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 愉快的时光总是如此短暂…您若在旅途中又遇到什么趣事，还请记得与我分享，好么？
t_en: Time flies when you're having fun... If you run into anything interesting on your travels, don't forget to share them with me, okay?

t_ch: 克劳迪娅专注地望着你。少女的明眸里似有群星闪烁，而你则是这片浩瀚银河的中心。
t_en: Claudia looks at you with piercing earnestness. Star clusters glitter in this girl's eyes, and you have become the center of this vast galaxy.

t_ch: 这就是无名客吗…能这般随心所欲地遨游星空，听起来可真浪漫。
t_en: So these are Nameless... To travel the stars to your hearts' desire, it sounds rather romantic.

t_ch: 要不是家里还有个不省心的哥哥和大堆产业要打理，我真想现在就请求和您一起去旅行。
t_en: If I didn't have a worry-wart brother and a ton of business to attend to, I would really like to ask you to take me with you.

t_ch: 虽然之前也时不时从博识学会的人手里收购些稀罕的消息作为写作素材，但那群学究死板得很，与您聊天就有意思多了。
t_en: While I occasionally get some rare bits of detail from the Intelligentsia Guild to use as material, those stuffy researchers really do bore me. It's much more fun to talk to you.

t_ch: 所以求求您啦，再多和我说一些您的故事吧？
t_en: So, could you please tell me a few more of your stories?

t_ch: 明媚的少女肉眼可见地晴转多云，整个人都蒙上一层雾霭般的忧郁。
t_en: The radiant girl's eyes turn clouded, her whole being is enveloped in a layer of misted melancholy.

t_ch: 为什么啊…为什么我的文字总是不够恐怖呢？
t_en: Why is that... Why can't my words evoke terror?

t_ch: 是场面描写不够血肉模糊吗？是剧情发展不够惊险刺激吗？是节奏不够紧张、气氛不够压抑吗？
t_en: Are my descriptions not visceral enough? Is my plot development not harrowing and thrilling enough? Is the pace lacking, the ambience not suppressive enough?

t_ch: 明明光是素材就已经收集了十几个书架了…难道我真的在这方面毫无天赋吗？
t_en: I've collected dozens of bookshelves worth of material... Do I really not have the talent for this?

t_ch: 少女的喉咙中滚出一声幼猫似的呜咽。
t_en: A kitten's mewling wail escapes the girl's throat.

t_ch: 明明、明明其它题材就没问题…明明就连以哥哥们为原型写的那些——唔，小说——都能大卖……
t_en: I—I obviously don't have problems with other themes... Even those novels written based *hic* on my older brothers' drafts were major hits...

t_ch: 那种诡谲的怪诞的压抑的疯狂的不可名状的…埃德加先生究竟是怎么做到的啊……
t_en: That creepy, grotesque, depressing, mad, unfathomable prose... How the heck did Mr. Edgar accomplish that...

t_ch: 为什么啊…为什么我的文字总是不够恐怖呢？
t_en: Why is that... Why can't my words evoke terror?

t_ch: 是场面描写不够血肉模糊吗？是剧情发展不够惊险刺激吗？是节奏不够紧张、气氛不够压抑吗？
t_en: Are my descriptions not visceral enough? Is my plot development not harrowing and thrilling enough? Is the pace lacking, the ambience not suppressive enough?

t_ch: 明明光是素材就已经收集了十几个书架了…难道我真的在这方面毫无天赋吗？
t_en: I've collected dozens of bookshelves worth of material... Do I really not have the talent for this?

t_ch: 少女的喉咙中滚出一声幼猫似的呜咽。
t_en: A kitten's mewling wail escapes the girl's throat.

t_ch: 明明、明明其它题材就没问题…明明就连以哥哥们为原型写的那些——唔，小说——都能大卖……
t_en: I—I obviously don't have problems with other themes... Even those novels written based *hic* on my older brothers' drafts were major hits...

t_ch: 那种诡谲的怪诞的压抑的疯狂的不可名状的…埃德加先生究竟是怎么做到的啊……
t_en: That creepy, grotesque, depressing, mad, unfathomable prose... How the heck did Mr. Edgar accomplish that...

t_ch: 少女的脸颊泛起薄红，眼睫低垂，神情中略带羞涩，娇艳之中平添几分动人。
t_en: The girl's face reddens, her eyelashes downcast, a glimpse of faint bashfulness in her demeanor — they only accentuate her delicate beauty.

t_ch: 所以说路易斯是■■的傻■吗？！为了个小画家追星追到匹诺康尼，现在好了，塌房了吧？
t_en: So is this Louis really some ■■ ■ moron or what!? He went all the way to Penacony to pursue some burgeoning painter. And look how it's turned out — it's all gone south now, hasn't it?

t_ch: 我■■放着手里一堆工作没做完，跨了好几个星系陪他「圆梦」就算了，现在还得收拾■■的烂摊子！
t_en: I ■■ dropped all my work and traversed several star systems to help him "fulfill his dream," and now I have to pick up the ■■ pieces!

t_ch: 我看大哥就是太惯着他了，多大的人了，还■■整天让我们给他擦屁股！每次看他那被踹了一脚的小狗似的脸我就气不打一处来！
t_en: You know what it is? Elder Brother dotes on him too much, that's what. He's a grown man who needs us to clean up his ■■ every other day! Every time I see that pathetic face of his, I have to remember to breathe from being so mad!

t_ch: 最后一次！这次一定得是最后一次！以后再管他我就是■■！
t_en: Last time! This is the very last time! If I clean up after him next time, I'll be the ■■!

t_ch: 眼前的少女像株饱蘸了阳光的向日葵，落在你眼里的影子仿佛都在闪闪发亮。
t_en: The girl in front of you is akin to a sunflower embraced in sunlight, the shadow cast over your eyes seemingly shimmering.

t_ch: 您对都市传说感兴趣吗？您看，像这样一个梦之城，必定会有很多迷人的秘密——
t_en: Are you interested in urban legends? Just like in this city of dreams, there must be countless mesmerizing secrets...

t_ch: 就比如…您听说过那个「梦境杀手」吗？据说它会以某种令人难以想象的方式收割梦中人的「灵魂」…可没人知道它究竟是什么样子。
t_en: For example... Have you heard of the Dreamscape Killer? People say it harvests souls in ways that words can't even describe... But no one knows what it is exactly.

t_ch: 如果有机会的话，您要不要和我一起调查？如果是您的话，或许真能找到些线索呢。
t_en: If the opportunity arises, do you want to investigate with me? I think we'd find some clues if you were around.

t_ch: 来嘛，这是后续调查的启动资金，请您务必收下哦。
t_en: Come on, this is the initial funding for the subsequent investigation. Please take it.

t_ch: 您对都市传说感兴趣吗？您看，像这样一座梦之城，必定会有很多迷人的秘密——
t_en: Are you interested in urban legends? Just like in this city of dreams, there must be countless mesmerizing secrets...

t_ch: 如果有机会的话，您要不要和我一起调查？如果是您的话，或许真能找到些线索呢。
t_en: If the opportunity arises, do you want to investigate with me? I think we'd find some clues if you were around.

t_ch: 衷心期盼您能为我带来些有趣的消息。
t_en: I'm really looking forward to you bringing me some interesting news.

t_ch: 站住！来，到我面前。这里是皮皮西人专属的俱乐部，你是皮皮西人吗？
t_en: Freeze! Come over here, to me. This is a Pepeshi-only club. Are you a Pepeshi?

t_ch: 我觉得我是。
t_en: I feel like I am.

t_ch: 哈。脸皮这么厚的家伙还是第一次见。脚并拢了，背挺直！不准弯膝盖！
t_en: Ha. First time anyone's tried pulling that one on me. Feet together, back straight! No bending your knees!

t_ch: 这位身高还不到门把手的皮皮西门卫在你的腰部比划了下，又掏出尺仔细量了量你的腿长。你突然很好奇她平时是如何打开大门的。
t_en: The Pepeshi guard, who stands no taller than the doorknob, makes a gesture at your waist before taking out a ruler to carefully measure the length of your legs. You're suddenly curious about how she manages to open the door.

t_ch: 超出标准线三根皮皮西手杖的高度，就这你还敢说自己是皮皮西人？
t_en: That's over the height limit by the length of three Pepeshi canes. And you still dare to say that you're a Pepeshi?

t_ch: 皮皮西人就了不起？
t_en: What's so great about the Pepeshi people?

t_ch: 那当然，皮皮西人就是了不起！我们是宇宙中最智慧的种族，也是最勤奋最有活力的人群！聪敏上进，真诚友善，和某些只知道凭身高显摆的生物完全不一样！
t_en: Because the Pepeshi ARE a great people! We're the most intelligent race in the whole universe, as well as the most hard-working and energetic! Smart, motivated, sincere, friendly — we're completely different from creatures who only know how to show off their height!

t_ch: …很抱歉，这里不欢迎你！请回吧。
t_en: ...My apologies, but you're not welcome here! Please get out.

t_ch: 再给你个建议！下次过来的时候，你可以试着蹲在地上和我讲话——说不定我一高兴就放你进去了。
t_en: Let me give you a suggestion! Next time you come here, try squatting down low on the ground and talking to me... Maybe I'll be in a good mood and let you slide.

t_ch: 你看我像皮皮西人吗？
t_en: Do I look like a Pepeshi to you?

t_ch: …别动。脚并拢了，背挺直！不准弯膝盖！站稳点，让我仔细瞧瞧。
t_en: ...Stand still! Feet together and back straight! Stay there and let me take a good look.

t_ch: 这位身高还不到门把手的皮皮西门卫在你的头顶比划了下，又掏出尺仔细量了量你的腿长，随后露出狐疑的神色。
t_en: The Pepeshi guard, who stands no taller than the doorknob, makes a gesture over your head before taking out a ruler to carefully measure the length of your legs, and then gives you a scrutinizing look.

t_ch: 没想到啊，梦境里居然能碰见比我还矮的人。我对你挺有好感的，可以交个朋友。
t_en: I didn't expect to meet someone shorter than me. To be honest, I'm quite fond of you. We can become friends.

t_ch: 可惜，你终究不是皮皮西人。按照规定，我不能让你入场。
t_en: But unfortunately, you are not a Pepeshi, and I'm not allowed to let you pass.

t_ch: …只要比你矮就能够进去吗？
t_en: ...As long as I'm shorter than you... you'll let me in?

t_ch: 这只是必要条件！其余种族的幼童，制造的机械生命，或是因特殊情况而达到身高标准的…都不得进入！规则很清晰，别想在我这里钻空子。
t_en: That's just the minimum requirement! Children of other species, artificial mechanical lifeforms, or those who are under the height limit due to special circumstances... none of them may enter, either! The rules are crystal clear, so don't try and pull any funny stuff.

t_ch: 谛听呢？
t_en: What about a Diting?

t_ch: 人为制造的东西当然不符合要求。这点刚才说得很清楚了。
t_en: Of course artificial entities won't meet the requirements. I already made that very clear.

t_ch: 呜呜伯呢？
t_en: What about a Wubbaboo?

t_ch: …星灵？它们没有实质形体，属于特殊情况，当然不符合要求。这点刚才说得很清楚了。
t_en: ...Astral Spirits? They have no physical form, so they're a special circumstance — of course they don't meet the requirements. I already made that very clear.

t_ch: 冰原熊幼崽呢？
t_en: What about arctic bear cubs?

t_ch: …别来跟我玩「幼崽」还是「幼童」的文字游戏，其余种族的幼年期都不行！这点刚才说得很清楚了。
t_en: ...Don't squabble with me over the definition of "cubs" and "children" — no juveniles of any species! I already made that very clear.

t_ch: 比你高的皮皮西人呢？
t_en: What about Pepeshi who are taller than you?

t_ch: 哼哼哼，我就猜到会有人提出这种问题。很遗憾地告诉你，在整个匹诺康尼，我就是最高的皮皮西人！不然你以为我是怎么得到这份工作的？
t_en: Hmmph, I knew someone would ask me that question. I regret to inform you that I'm the tallest Pepeshi in all of Penacony! How else do you think I got to work this gig?

t_ch: 那我呢？
t_en: What about me?

t_ch: 别瞎起哄！继续捣乱的话，我就要联系猎犬家系了。
t_en: Stop trying to stir up drama! If you keep doing this, I'll have to call the Bloodhound Family over.

t_ch: 我再专门给你解释一遍——只有「皮皮西人」，才有资格进入「皮皮西俱乐部」！这样总能听懂了吧？
t_en: Let me explain this: Only the Pepeshi people are entitled to enter the Pepeshi Club! Do you understand now?

t_ch: 要是我把梦境形象换成皮皮西…
t_en: What if I changed my Dreamscape avatar to a Pepeshi...

t_ch: 除目测外，我们还会检查访客的梦境护照，核对种族信息和出身地，确保不会有假扮成皮皮西人混进来的情况出现。劝你少打些歪主意。
t_en: Besides visual observations, we also check the visitor's Dreamscape Pass to verify their ethnic data and place of origin, so that nobody can enter while impersonating a Pepeshi. I advise you to stop coming up with crooked schemes.

t_ch: 要是皮皮西人把梦境形象换成…
t_en: What if a Pepeshi person changed their dreamscape avatar...

t_ch: 为保障所有会员的正当权益…根据规定，只要梦境护照登记的资料能证明是位皮皮西客人，就允许进入俱乐部……
t_en: To protect the rights of all club members... based on regulations, if Dreamscape Pass credentials are able to prove that you are a Pepeshi visitor, then entry into the club will be permitted...

t_ch: 就是说，任何外观都可以进入。
t_en: Which is to say, any appearance is allowed in.

t_ch: 从刚才的推论来看…是的，但…很少…几乎不会有特殊情况……
t_en: Taking this to its logical conclusion, then... Yes, but... very few... almost no special circumstances...

t_ch: 几乎…不会有……
t_en: Almost... never...

t_ch: 你怎么知道我不是皮皮西人？
t_en: How would you know if I'm not Pepeshi?

t_ch: 你居然敢假定我的种族？
t_en: Did you just assume my race?

t_ch: 因为我的工作失误，给您造成了困扰。向您道歉了，真的很对不起。
t_en: I'm so sorry for this misunderstanding. I apologize and am very sorry.

t_ch: <size=28>——这样总行了吧！！！没其他事就赶快走！</size>
t_en: <size=28>...Is that good enough!? If you've got nothing else to say, please go!</size>

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: …哈？我没听错吧？…你敢把这句话对着匹诺康尼的所有皮皮西人都说一遍吗？
t_en: ...Huh? Did I hear that right? ...I dare you to say that again in front of all the Pepeshi people in Penacony to hear.

t_ch: 我当然敢。
t_en: Of course, I will.

t_ch: 说到做到。
t_en: I keep my promises.

t_ch: 好！别忘了你的话！你这无礼之徒，等着被皮皮西勾拳揍得鼻青脸肿吧！
t_en: Alright, rude! Don't forget what you said! Just wait until you're beaten black and blue by a Pepeshi right hook!

t_ch: 我对你们的俱乐部没有兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested in joining your club.

t_ch: 那就赶快走！别挡着我看街景了——就是说，长得高不意味着能为所欲为！
t_en: Then clear out! Don't block my view of the street — I mean, just because you're tall doesn't mean you can just do whatever you want!

t_ch: 切诺对你露出了鄙夷的眼神，仿佛在说：「你怎么还在？」
t_en: Cielo flashes a disgusted look at you as if to say, "What are you still doing here?"

t_ch: 这里是皮皮西人专属的俱乐部！比我高的生物一律不得入内！
t_en: This is an exclusive club for Pepeshi only! No creature taller than me may enter!

t_ch: 要是持有贵宾卡也可以…你有吗？哼，看你就没有。
t_en: You can if you've got a VIP card... Do you? Hmm, you don't.

t_ch: 这位尽职尽责的门卫将脸深深埋进掌心，像是要落下泪来。
t_en: The diligent guard buries her face deeply into her palms as if she were about to weep.

t_ch: 呜…没想到有天，皮皮西人的身份…会让我感到如此地悲伤。
t_en: *Sob*... I never thought I'd see the day that my identity as a Pepeshi... would leave me feeling so depressed.

t_ch: 我曾在梦境中有过美妙的邂逅，对方…是我在车站前遇到的一位皮皮西绅士，他满足我对爱情的一切幻想，英俊、博学、温柔而专情。毫无疑问，我迅速迷上了他。
t_en: I once had a wonderful experience in the dreamscape. It was with... a Pepeshi gentleman I met before the train station. He fulfilled all of my romantic fantasies: Handsome, smart, gentle, and dedicated. Without a doubt, I instantly fell for him.

t_ch: 我向他递出了加入俱乐部的邀请，他欣然赴约。但…他的现实身份，居然是步离人！
t_en: I gave him an invitation to come and join the club, and he gladly accepted. But... who would've known his true identity is a borisin!

t_ch: 不可能的，我没法接受一位非皮皮西的伴侣，可是我…我没法控制不去想他。种族该成为爱情必须考量的因素吗，不该成为吗？我不知道……
t_en: That's impossible. I could never accept a non-Pepeshi partner. But I... I couldn't stop myself from thinking about him. Should race be a factor when it comes to love, or should it not? I have no idea...

t_ch: 只是…他无法忍受我的摇摆不定，最终选择了离开。呵，这就是…门卫的宿命吧。
t_en: I just know... that he couldn't stand all my dithering about and in the end chose to leave. *sigh* This is... the fate of all guards.

t_ch: 恪守自己的职责，最终将爱情也拒之门外……
t_en: Abide by your duty, and in the end, shut out any doorway to love...

t_ch: 切诺挥舞起了手臂，似乎正身处在盛大而狂热的游行中。
t_en: Cielo waves her hand, as if in a majestic and fantastic demonstration.

t_ch: 这个社会对皮皮西人未免也太不友好了！
t_en: This society is too unfriendly to us Pepeshi!

t_ch: 所有设施都是按照常规体型修建的，根本没有考虑到皮皮西人使用时的情况。酒吧的高脚椅、礼堂的阶梯、甚至汽水瓶的尺寸，对皮皮西人来说用起来都很不方便！
t_en: All facilities are built based on conventional body types, without considering use cases for the Pepeshi people. Bar stools, lobby steps, and even the size of soda bottles are inconvenient for the Pepeshi people!

t_ch: 这就是体系化的歧视！我前天过马路时差点被球笼给撞了，结果那个司机却说我身高太矮，后视镜里看不到——这说明什么？这种歧视已经严重威胁到了全体皮皮西的生命安全！
t_en: This is systemic discrimination! I was almost hit by a spheroid while crossing the road a couple of days ago, but the driver somehow told me I was too short to be seen in the rearview mirror — what does that mean? This kind of discrimination gravely threatens the health and safety of the Pepeshi people!

t_ch: …强烈抗议！皮皮西人是匹诺康尼的基石，你们这是在自毁根基！
t_en: ...It's an outrage! The Pepeshi people form the cornerstone of Penacony, and this is destroying your own bedrock!

t_ch: 切诺欢快地掰起了手指头，恍若流连在迷幻的颂歌中。
t_en: Cielo counts her fingers happily, as if immersed in an illusory hymn.

t_ch: 现在请听好了！——关于「皮皮西人为什么是神」的本体论证明。
t_en: Listen up! Here is the evidential proof behind the theory that "the Pepeshi people are gods":

t_ch: …皮皮西人是全宇宙最伟大的种族。
t_en: ...The Pepeshi people are the greatest species in the universe.

t_ch: …皮皮西人是全宇宙最可爱的种族。
t_en: ...The Pepeshi people are the cutest species in the universe.

t_ch: …皮皮西人是全宇宙最聪明的种族。
t_en: ...The Pepeshi people are the smartest species in the universe.

t_ch: …皮皮西人是全宇宙最勤奋的种族。
t_en: ... Pepeshi people are the most hard-working species in the universe.

t_ch: …皮皮西人是全宇宙最正义……
t_en: ...The Pepeshi people are the most righteous species in...

t_ch: 抢在切诺说出对皮皮西人的第五句赞美之前，你赶紧逃开了。
t_en: You quickly run off before Cielo can finish saying her fifth line proselytizing the greatness of the Pepeshi people.
...Groo?

t_ch: 小冰原熊歪着脑袋打量着你，扑扇着两只半圆形的耳朵。
t_en: The little arctic bear cub watches you with its head tilted, flapping its semicircular ears.

t_ch: 你很确信，没有人能拒绝一只毛绒绒的可爱生物，更何况，它正挥动着又短又圆的前肢，似乎想给你一个小小的熊抱。
t_en: You're certain no one can ever resist a cute, fluffy critter — not to mention that it's waving its cute little stumpy arms at you as if to ask for a little bear hug.

t_ch: …你忍心拒绝吗？
t_en: ...How could you say no?

t_ch: 顺从心中的欲念。
t_en: Submit to your heart's desire.

t_ch: 放弃吧，你只不过是犯了任何人在面对这只熊崽时都会犯下的错误。
t_en: Give up. You're just making the same mistake anyone else would when faced with a bear cub like this.

t_ch: 你悄声走上前，很好，熊崽没有跑开，仍旧用无辜的眼神望着你。你已经开始想象那柔软的皮毛在掌心中的触感了，你相信那就是你始终在追寻的幸福。
t_en: You walk forward, quietly. Good: It hasn't run off but is still watching you with those innocent eyes. You begin to imagine the soft touch of that fur in the palm of your hand and wonder: Is this the happiness that you've been pursuing all along?

t_ch: 这时…你的余光似乎瞥到了什么。
t_en: Just then... you seem to catch a glimpse of something out of the corner of your eyes.

t_ch: 某个绝对不该出现于此的东西。它的存在是如此刺眼，就像汽水瓶口的弹珠，衬衫领上的口红印，以至于你无法忽视。
t_en: Something that has absolutely no business being here. Its presence is so offensive — like marbles in a soda bottle, or lipstick on a shirt collar — that it's impossible to ignore.

t_ch: 你的动作停住了。
t_en: Your movements stop.

t_ch: ……咕呜？
t_en: ...Groohoo?

t_ch: 顺从心中的欲念。
t_en: Submit to your heart's desire.

t_ch: 管它的！能有什么比此刻的幸福更重要。哪怕{RUBY_B#蟑螂}「繁育」的余孽{RUBY_E#}即将率全族进攻匹诺康尼，你都决定要先将温软的皮毛抓在手中。你确定这就是你愿为之付出所有的正义。
t_en: Who cares about anyone else! Nothing is more important than happiness right here, right now. Even if the {RUBY_B#Cockroach}dregs of Propagation {RUBY_E#} are about to lead a withering assault against Penacony, you decide you want to grab onto that soft fluff first. You're sure that this is the great justice behind why you do all you do.

t_ch: 你的手指张开，指尖微微颤抖。很好，你就要触碰到它了。
t_en: Your hand spreads wide, fingers quivering slightly. Good, you're about to pet it.

t_ch: …你确定不再考虑下吗？
t_en: ...Are you sure you don't want to think about it some more?

t_ch: 咕呜…咕？
t_en: Groohoo... Groo?

t_ch: 顺从心中的欲念。
t_en: Submit to your heart's desire.

t_ch: 你赢了！你很勇敢，没人能阻拦你了。尽情去做想做的一切吧！
t_en: You win! You are bold, and none can resist you. Go ahead and do whatever you want!

t_ch: 你的手掌紧紧抱住了小熊。比预想得要重一些，但没关系，柔顺的毛发挠得你手心发痒，也让你的心头充满了暖意。你的人生从此又多了一个美妙的时刻。
t_en: Your palm hugs onto the little cub tightly. It's a little heavier than you expected, but that doesn't matter — its soft fur tickles the palm of your hands while filling your heart up to the brim with warmth. Another beautiful life moment for you.

t_ch: 呜！咕呜呜！
t_en: Groo! Groooo!

t_ch: 它发出了开心的叫声，看起来对你无礼的举动并未感到冒犯。
t_en: It grunts happily and doesn't seem to be offended by your rude behavior.

t_ch: 你松了口气。冒险很成功，你得到了想要的东西。那…代价是什么？
t_en: You sigh with relief. The risk paid off, and you got what you wanted. But... at what cost?

t_ch: 等等…这不对劲。
t_en: Hang on... something isn't right.

t_ch: 等等…这不对劲。
t_en: Hang on... something isn't right.

t_ch: 等等…这不对劲。
t_en: Hang on... something isn't right.

t_ch: …你终于意识到了不对劲？
t_en: ...You only just realized something isn't right?

t_ch: …你总算从盲目的冲动中回过神来了？
t_en: ...Have you recovered yet from the rashness of your blind impulse?

t_ch: 难道你最开始就没有注意到，眼前这个生物头顶挂着的名字根本不是「冰原熊幼崽」么？
t_en: Didn't you notice that this whole time, the sign hanging above the creature doesn't say "Arctic Bear Cub" at all?
...Groo?

t_ch: 深呼吸，别急，至少对方目前没有生气，你还有充足的时间来思考对策。
t_en: Take a deep breath and don't get excited. At least they aren't angry right now, and you still have plenty of time to think about mitigation strategies.

t_ch: 解释刚才的行为。
t_en: Explain what you just did.

t_ch: 既然对方选择以冰原熊幼崽的模样示人，用相应的方法来接触肯定不会错。因此，你决定……
t_en: Seeing as they have chosen to display themselves as an arctic bear cub, it's not technically wrong to use a corresponding method of petting. Therefore, you decide to...

t_ch: 要不还是赶紧跑路吧。
t_en: Maybe we should just scuttle.

t_ch: 若无其事地离开。
t_en: Leave as if nothing happened.

t_ch: 呼…呼呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 在你徒劳无益地寻找对策时，冰原熊幼崽自顾自地睡着了！
t_en: While you're desperately searching for a solution, the little cub falls asleep!

t_ch: 好吧，看来这位，呃……「动物爱好者」并不介意被当作动物对待。
t_en: Fine. Look at this, er... the "animal enthusiast" doesn't mind being treated like an animal, after all.
...Groo?

t_ch: 呜！咕呜呜！
t_en: Groo! Groooo!
Woo...
Woo...

t_ch: 怎么回事？在你校准对方情绪的一瞬间，一只呜呜伯突然出现在了眼前。
t_en: What's going on? The moment you calibrate the target's mood, a Wubbaboo pops out of nowhere.

t_ch: 呜呜，呜呜呜。
t_en: Woo-woo, woo whoooo woo.

t_ch: 呜！
t_en: Woo!

t_ch: 呜呜伯自顾自地围绕你飘浮起来，速度越来越快，越来越快，直到……
t_en: The Wubbaboo minds its own business, hovering around you, circling faster, faster, and faster, until...

t_ch: 我的梦境护照呢？！
t_en: My Dreamscape Pass?

t_ch: 呜呜。
t_en: Woo-woo.

t_ch: 你眼睁睁地看着你的梦境护照如同风中的落叶般逐渐飘远，然后……
t_en: You watch as your Dreamscape Pass drifts away from you like a leaf in the wind. And then...

t_ch: 不偏不倚地落进了某个路人敞开的背包里。
t_en: It stages a perfect landing straight into the open inventory of a passerby.

t_ch: 唔咕，找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Uh-oh, what do you want from me?

t_ch: 我的东西在你的包里。
t_en: Something of mine is in your backpack.

t_ch: …真的吗？这不是什么新型诈骗吧……
t_en: ...Really? Is this some kind of new scam...

t_ch: 我看看…唔，居然还真有？这就是你在找的东西吧？
t_en: Let me see... Oh, there actually is something? Is that yours?

t_ch: 喏，拿去吧。它理应属于你。
t_en: Here, take it. It's rightfully yours.

t_ch: 不知为何，对方将整个背包都递给了你。
t_en: For some reason, the other person gives you their entire inventory.

t_ch: 确认你没有其余意图后，眼前的皮皮西人瞬间跑没影了。
t_en: After confirming you have no ulterior intentions, the Pepeshi person vanishes in an instant.

t_ch: （是不是搞错了……）
t_en: (Was that a mistake...)

t_ch: 呜？呜呜！
t_en: Woo? Woo-woo!

t_ch: 你无法再容忍这只调皮捣蛋的呜呜伯了，它就该被关在空间站的收容舱里！活活关上整整一百万个琥珀纪！
t_en: You can no longer tolerate this cheeky, mischievous Wubbaboo. It should be locked up in the space station's containment cabin! For a million Amber Eras!

t_ch: 趁它还没有想到新的恶作剧方法时，赶紧溜走吧。
t_en: Sneak off before it comes up with a new way to prank you.

t_ch: 我的手机呢？！
t_en: Where's my phone?

t_ch: 呜呜。
t_en: Woo-woo.

t_ch: 你眼睁睁地看着你的手机屏幕兀自亮了起来，嗒嗒的按键声响起，然后……
t_en: You watch helplessly on as the screen on your phone lights up and the keys start clacking. Then...

t_ch: 拨通了某个你很熟悉的号码。
t_en: It dials through to the number of someone you know very well.

t_ch: 星际和平公司，您的订单已收到。商品将于三个匹诺康尼日内送达，请……
t_en: Welcome to IPC. your order has been received. Items will be delivered within three Penacony days. Please...

t_ch: 我是未成年人，我要退款！
t_en: I'm a minor. I want a refund!

t_ch: 噢，很遗憾听到这个消息…但您的商品已经寄出，拦截非常困难，除非——
t_en: Oh, I'm sorry to hear that... but your item has already been shipped and cannot be intercepted, unless...

t_ch: 除非…您能帮忙消耗掉我们的库存。
t_en: Unless... you can help deplete our inventory.

t_ch: 看起来你别无选择。
t_en: It looks like you don't have any choice.

t_ch: （咦，订单显示的商品是5个「仰望星空」……）
t_en: (Hey now, the order is for five "Stargazer"...)

t_ch: 呜？呜呜！
t_en: Woo? Woo-woo!

t_ch: 你无法再容忍这只调皮捣蛋的呜呜伯了，它就该被关在空间站的收容舱里！活活关上整整一百万个琥珀纪！
t_en: You can no longer tolerate this cheeky, mischievous Wubbaboo. It should be locked up in the space station's containment cabin! For a million Amber Eras!

t_ch: 趁它还没有想到新的恶作剧方法时，赶紧溜走吧。
t_en: Sneak off before it comes up with a new way to prank you.
Woo...

t_ch: 在你校准对方情绪的一瞬间，一只呜呜伯突然出现在了眼前。
t_en: The moment you calibrate the target's mood, a Wubbaboo pops out of nowhere.

t_ch: 呜呜，呜呜呜。
t_en: Woo-woo, woo whoooo woo.

t_ch: 呜！
t_en: Woo!

t_ch: 呜呜伯自顾自地围绕你飘浮起来，速度越来越快，越来越快，直到……
t_en: The Wubbaboo minds its own business, hovering around you, circling faster, faster, and faster, until...

t_ch: 犹豫就会败北！
t_en: You snooze you lose!

t_ch: 你不会再给这只呜呜伯任何捉弄你的机会。趁它还在转圈的时候，你迅速溜走了。
t_en: You won't give this Wubbaboo another chance to trick you again. You sneak off while it's still spinning around.

t_ch: 汪，汪汪！
t_en: Woof, ruff ruff!

t_ch: 汪，汪汪！
t_en: Woof, ruff ruff!

t_ch: 怎么回事？在你校准对方情绪的一瞬间，一只谛听突然出现在了眼前！
t_en: What's going on? The moment you calibrate the target's mood, a Diting pops out of nowhere.

t_ch: 汪汪…汪…汪……
t_en: Woof woof... woof.. ruff...

t_ch: 谛听从喉咙深处发出微弱的呜咽声，不住用头蹭着你的腿，像极了只刚被雨淋过的小狗。
t_en: The Diting makes a weak whimper from deep within its gullet and keeps rubbing its head against your leg just like a puppy drenched in a downpour.

t_ch: 好吧，每个人都有被认可的需求，你确实觉得这种感觉…还不错。
t_en: Fine, everyone has a need for validation. You come to realize that this feeling... really is not bad.

t_ch: 拍拍它的额头。
t_en: Pat it on the head.

t_ch: 挠挠它的喉咙。
t_en: Scratch its throat.

t_ch: 摸摸它的肚子。
t_en: Rub its belly.

t_ch: 汪！汪汪汪……
t_en: Woof! Woof ruff ruff...

t_ch: 谛听嗅了嗅你的手指，随后咬住了你的鞋带，轻轻拉扯着，似乎想带你去某个地方。
t_en: The Diting sniffs your fingers and then nibbles on your shoelace, softly pulling at it as though dragging you off somewhere else.

t_ch: 去看看吧。
t_en: Go take a look.

t_ch: 带我走吧。
t_en: Take me with you.

t_ch: 你决定听从谛听的指引，毕竟它曾经帮过你不少忙。不巧，你是个懂得感恩的人。
t_en: You decide to follow the Diting's lead. After all, it has already helped you to no end. As luck would have it, you're someone who understands what gratitude means.

t_ch: 汪…汪汪。汪！
t_en: Woof... ruff ruff. Woof!

t_ch: 谛听没有跑出多远，反而在街角的阴影处转悠了起来，随后叼回来…一袋垃圾。
t_en: The Diting hasn't made it that far before it starts loitering in the shadow at the street corner and later returns with... a bag of garbage.

t_ch: 它蹲在你的脚边，仍用天真无辜又带些可怜的眼神望着你。
t_en: It sits near your feet, still looking up at you with those innocent eyes.

t_ch: 汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof!

t_ch: 果然，匹诺康尼就不该有谛听存在。
t_en: Indeed, Ditings have no business being in Penacony.

t_ch: 汪，汪汪！
t_en: Woof, ruff ruff!

t_ch: 在你校准对方情绪的一瞬间，一只谛听突然出现在了眼前。
t_en: The moment you calibrate the target's mood, a Diting pops out of nowhere.

t_ch: 汪汪…汪…汪……
t_en: Woof woof... woof.. ruff...

t_ch: 谛听从喉咙深处发出微弱的呜咽声，不住用头蹭着你的腿，像极了只刚被雨淋过的小狗。
t_en: The Diting makes a weak whimper from deep within its gullet and keeps rubbing its head against your leg just like a puppy drenched in a downpour.

t_ch: 好吧，每个人都有被认可的需求，你确实觉得这种感觉…还不错。
t_en: Fine, everyone has a need for validation. You come to realize that this feeling... really is not bad.

t_ch: 拍拍它的额头。
t_en: Pat it on the head.

t_ch: 挠挠它的喉咙。
t_en: Scratch its throat.

t_ch: 摸摸它的肚子。
t_en: Rub its belly.

t_ch: 汪！汪汪汪……
t_en: Woof! Woof ruff ruff...

t_ch: 谛听嗅了嗅你的手指，随后咬住了你的鞋带，轻轻拉扯着，似乎想把你带去某个地方。
t_en: The Diting sniffs your fingers and then nibbles on your shoelace, softly pulling at it as if wanting to drag you off somewhere else.

t_ch: 我的意志坚如钢铁。
t_en: My resolve remains as strong as steel.

t_ch: 你很清楚，匹诺康尼不可能出现*正版的*谛听。
t_en: You know very well that there cannot be a *genuine* Diting in Penacony.

t_ch: 靠旁边站点，别影响我表演！
t_en: Move aside and don't ruin my performance!

t_ch: 真是服了你们这些人类！
t_en: I really have to hand it to you humans!

t_ch: 怎么回事？在你校准对方情绪的一瞬间，一名智械女性突然出现在了眼前！
t_en: What's going on? The moment you calibrate the target's mood, an Intellitron girl appears out of nowhere.

t_ch: 看什么看！没见过和你不一样的生物吗？！
t_en: What are you looking at? Have you never seen a creature that's different from you before!?

t_ch: 我最瞧不起那些搞区别对待的人！帝皇战争之后，我们智械经过了多少纪的斗争，才赢得和有机生命对等的地位？
t_en: I hate people who discriminate the most! Do you know how many eras of struggle it took for us Intellitrons to be emancipated and granted equal status with living species after the Emperor's War?

t_ch: 我能出现在这里，就说明那套什么…灵魂…人性分类法，根本就是胡扯！
t_en: The very fact that I can stand here is testimony to the fact that such concepts as a soul... as humanity... these classifications are simply nonsense!

t_ch: 无机生命好不容易争取到了入梦的权利——那其它动物呢？熊？谛听？呜呜伯？凭什么那些小生命就不能获得入梦权？它们难道不配享有这片乐园吗？
t_en: Inorganic life has finally wrested the right to dream for ourselves — but what about other creatures? Bears? Ditings? Wubbaboos? Why can't those other life forms enjoy the right to dream? Do they not also deserve a little slice of this paradise, too?

t_ch: 就因为你们的偏见，才导致这城市如此匮乏生命力…才让这大街上满是人的臭味！
t_en: It's your prejudice that's making this city so lacking in life... that's making the streets stink of humans!

t_ch: 再次警告你，不准歧视任何生物！就算是一只穿街过巷的太空鼠，它的生命也和你是平等的！少来和我杠！
t_en: Let me warn you again, do not discriminate against any creature! Even if it's just a space rat crossing the street, its life is just as precious as yours! So stop trying to get smart with me!

t_ch: 没打算和你杠。
t_en: I wasn't going to.

t_ch: 其实谛听是人造的…
t_en: Actually, Ditings are artificial...
